2604.09085.title: Beyond Isolated Clients: Integrating Graph-Based Embeddings into Event Sequence Models
2604.09085.paper_summary: Large-scale digital platforms generate billions of timestamped user-item interactions (events) that are crucial for predicting user attributes in, e.g., fraud prevention and recommendations. While self-supervised learning (SSL) effectively models the temporal order of events, it typically overlooks the global structure of the user-item interaction graph. To bridge this gap, we propose three model-agnostic strategies for integrating this structural information into contrastive SSL: enriching event embeddings, aligning client representations with graph embeddings, and adding a structural pretext task. Experiments on four financial and e-commerce datasets demonstrate that our approach consistently improves the accuracy (up to a 2.3% AUC) and reveals that graph density is a key factor in selecting the optimal integration strategy.
2604.09085.paper_summary_zh: 大型數位平台生成數十億個帶時間戳的用戶-項目互動（事件），這對於預測用戶屬性（例如，防詐騙和推薦）至關重要。雖然自我監督學習（SSL）有效地建模事件的時間順序，但它通常忽略了用戶-項目互動圖的全局結構。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了三種與模型無關的策略，以將這些結構信息整合到對比SSL中：豐富事件嵌入、將客戶端表示與圖嵌入對齊，以及添加結構性前置任務。在四個金融和電子商務數據集上的實驗表明，我們的方法始終提高了準確性（最高可達2.3%的AUC），並揭示了圖密度是選擇最佳整合策略的關鍵因素。
2604.09085.author: Harry Proshian et.al.
2604.09085.authors: Harry Proshian, Nikita Severin, Sergey Nikolenko, Kireev Ivan, Andrey Savchenko, Ivan Sergeev, Maria Postnova, Ilya Makarov
